faq.question: What time is it in Cairo right now?
faq.answer: Cairo operates on Eastern European Time (EET/UTC+2) year-round. Egypt permanently adopted UTC+2 in 2015 after discontinuing daylight saving time, making Cairo 7 hours ahead of New York (EST), 2 hours ahead of London (GMT), and 6 hours behind Tokyo (JST). This consistent timezone eliminates seasonal clock changes, simplifying international scheduling with Egypt's capital.

faq.question: What time zone is Cairo in?
faq.answer: Cairo uses Eastern European Time (EET), which is UTC+2 throughout the entire year. Since Egypt discontinued daylight saving time in 2015, Cairo maintains a permanent UTC+2 offset without seasonal adjustments. This timezone is shared with cities like Johannesburg, Athens, Helsinki, and Jerusalem, creating a stable time corridor across Africa, Europe, and the Middle East.

faq.question: Does Cairo observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Egypt discontinued daylight saving time in 2015 and Cairo now maintains Eastern European Time (EET/UTC+2) year-round. The Egyptian government decided to permanently adopt UTC+2 after years of switching between EET (UTC+2) and EEST (UTC+3), citing energy savings and public preference for consistent time. This makes Cairo one of the few major capitals that never changes its clocks.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Cairo and New York?
faq.answer: Cairo is 7 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (November-March) and 6 hours ahead during Eastern Daylight Time (March-November). Since Egypt no longer observes DST, the time gap changes when the US switches clocks: a 12pm meeting in Cairo equals 5am EST or 6am EDT in New York. Use our time converter at /time/cairo/new-york/ to calculate exact differences for your scheduling needs.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Cairo and London?
faq.answer: Cairo is 2 hours ahead of London during Greenwich Mean Time (GMT/October-March) and 1 hour ahead during British Summer Time (BST/March-October). When it's noon in Cairo, it's 10am GMT or 11am BST in London. This narrow time gap makes Cairo-London business collaboration highly efficient, with 6-7 hours of overlapping business hours daily. Check current difference at /time/cairo/london/.

faq.question: What time does the stock exchange open in Cairo?
faq.answer: The Egyptian Exchange (EGX) opens at 10:00 AM Cairo time (EET/UTC+2) and closes at 2:30 PM, Sunday through Thursday. Trading sessions run 10:00-10:30 AM (opening auction), 10:30 AM-2:00 PM (continuous trading), and 2:00-2:30 PM (closing auction). Egypt's financial week runs Sunday-Thursday, aligning with Middle Eastern markets. For international traders, EGX opening hours are 3:00 AM EST / 8:00 AM GMT.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Cairo from the US?
faq.answer: The best time to call Cairo from the US is 1:00-4:00 PM EST (8:00-11:00 PM Cairo time) or 10:00 AM-1:00 PM EST (5:00-8:00 PM Cairo time) for evening conversations. For business calls, target 3:00-8:00 AM EST (10:00 AM-3:00 PM Cairo time) to reach offices during working hours. From the West Coast, call 12:00-5:00 AM PST for Cairo business hours, or 10:00 AM-1:00 PM PST for evening contact.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Cairo?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Cairo are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM Sunday through Thursday, with many offices operating 8:30 AM to 3:30 PM. Government offices typically run 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM. Egypt's weekend is Friday-Saturday, with Thursday being the last business day. During Ramadan, hours shift dramatically to 10:00 AM-3:00 PM or 9:00 AM-2:00 PM. Banks operate 8:30 AM-2:00 PM Sunday-Thursday, with some branches offering extended hours until 5:00 PM.

seo_description: Current time in Cairo, Egypt. Eastern European Time (EET/UTC+2) year-round. No DST. Time differences, business hours, and timezone guide.
seo_title: Current Time in Cairo, Egypt — EET Timezone & UTC+2 Offset

content_blocks.title: Cairo Time Zone Guide: Eastern European Time (EET/UTC+2)
content_blocks.content: Cairo operates exclusively on Eastern European Time (EET), maintaining a permanent UTC+2 offset throughout the entire year. In 2015, Egypt made the strategic decision to discontinue daylight saving time after decades of seasonal clock changes, citing energy efficiency studies and public preference for time consistency. This makes Cairo one of the most scheduling-friendly major capitals globally, as international partners never need to adjust for Egyptian clock changes. The UTC+2 timezone places Cairo in the same time corridor as Johannesburg, Athens, Helsinki, Kyiv, and Jerusalem, creating seamless coordination across African, European, and Middle Eastern business hubs. For North American businesses, Cairo sits 7 hours ahead of New York during EST (winter) and 6 hours ahead during EDT (summer), while European partners enjoy a narrow 1-2 hour gap with London and a 1-hour difference from Paris and Berlin. Asian markets find Cairo 6 hours behind Tokyo, 5 hours behind Singapore, and 2 hours behind Dubai. Calculate exact time differences with our comparison tool at /time/cairo/new-york/ or explore scheduling options across multiple timezones using our meeting planner at /meeting/. Egypt's stable timezone policy has made Cairo a preferred hub for multinational operations requiring consistent cross-continental scheduling.

content_blocks.title: Business Hours and Working Week in Cairo
content_blocks.content: Cairo operates on a Sunday-Thursday business week, aligning with Middle Eastern and North African standards rather than the Western Monday-Friday model. Standard office hours run 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, though many businesses adopt 8:30 AM to 3:30 PM schedules to avoid afternoon heat. Government ministries and public sector offices typically operate shorter hours, from 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM Sunday through Thursday. The Egyptian banking sector maintains consistent hours of 8:30 AM to 2:00 PM, with select branches in commercial districts extending service until 5:00 PM to accommodate business clients. The Egyptian Exchange (EGX) operates 10:00 AM to 2:30 PM Cairo time, providing 4.5 hours of trading across three sessions. International businesses scheduling with Cairo benefit from substantial overlap: 6-7 hours with European markets (London, Paris, Frankfurt), 3-4 hours with US East Coast operations, and 2-3 hours with Gulf Cooperation Council countries. During the Islamic holy month of Ramadan, business hours shift dramatically to accommodate fasting, typically running 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM or 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM with reduced productivity in afternoon hours. Many multinational corporations in Cairo adopt flexible working arrangements during Ramadan. Use our international meeting scheduler at /meeting/ to find optimal calling times, or check real-time business hour overlap with /time/cairo/london/ for European coordination and /time/cairo/dubai/ for regional scheduling across the Middle East.

content_blocks.title: Calling Cairo: Optimal Times for International Communication
content_blocks.content: Successfully reaching contacts in Cairo requires understanding both timezone mathematics and cultural business patterns. From the US East Coast, the ideal business calling window is 3:00-8:00 AM EST (10:00 AM-3:00 PM Cairo time), catching Egyptian offices during peak productivity hours before the US workday begins. West Coast callers should target midnight-5:00 AM PST for business hours, or consider evening calls at 10:00 AM-1:00 PM PST (8:00-11:00 PM Cairo time) for personal contacts. European callers enjoy exceptional flexibility: London-based professionals can reach Cairo from 8:00 AM-3:00 PM GMT (10:00 AM-5:00 PM Cairo time), while Paris and Berlin operate nearly parallel hours with just a 1-hour offset. For casual or family calls from North America, late afternoon US time (1:00-4:00 PM EST / 10:00 AM-1:00 PM PST) corresponds to Cairo's evening hours (8:00-11:00 PM), when most people are home from work. Asian business centers face inverse challenges: Tokyo operates 6 hours ahead, making 3:00-8:00 PM JST optimal for reaching Cairo morning hours, while Singapore callers at 4:00-9:00 PM SGT catch Cairo's 10:00 AM-3:00 PM window. Remember that Egypt's weekend runs Friday-Saturday, so Thursday is the final business day of the week. Our timezone converter at /time/cairo/new-york/ provides precise calculation for any date, accounting for US DST changes that affect the hour gap, while /time/cairo/london/ helps European businesses coordinate across the narrow EET-GMT/BST difference. For complex multi-timezone scheduling involving Cairo, our meeting planner at /meeting/ identifies overlapping availability across up to 12 global locations simultaneously.
